reviews.review_text: Close to National Park at the bottom of Yorke Peninsula.
Nice little spot, wasnt busy-just after easter weekend.
Lovely park fantastic enclosed camp kitchen and undercover BBQ area with tables and chairs very close to beach just meters away and fishing was good for mullet toilets very clean
Friendly staff great park to stay in
Misschien hadden we pech, maar er was heel veel wind. De eerste gelegenheid om te eten is 1 km stappen als je geen auto hebt. De camp kitchen is wel dik in orde. Kleine speeltuin en een schaakspel.
Very relaxing lawn site amazing no shoes beeded.,Managers are very welcoming & friendly great hosts will be back. Highly recommend 20/10

Awesome place very clean ,amazing camp kitchen, great value for money, close to heaps of places to explore and fish ,even with this algae bloom thing goin on still managed to get tommys off the stenhouse bay jetty and further down the road makrel off what i called death rocks at pondalowi bay if ya game ,,heaps of Tommy's too but hard to get em up the ledge without em gettin off ,hot tip tho,  stock up! ,its pretty remote there is a store there with everything ya need food bait and a pub so you will survive, a truly amazing part of the country to escape from the rest of the world, i will definitely return one day

What a lovely caravan park perfectly situated on the beach. Our site was perhaps 10 steps to the sand and during our stay over the long weekend in 40 degrees was great for a quick dip to cool off. Amenities were clean and tidy as was the park itself. Bonus Saturday sourdough bread delivery at reception, kindly notified via a text. Dealing over the phone to make our booking was made with Don who went above and beyond to accommodate us. He was informed and patient and friendly. We definitely will return and didn’t want to leave.
Friendly staff, amazing facilities, clean and just 2 steps from the beach!

We stayed at the beautiful Marion bay caravan park for a week over Christmas was absolutely beautiful the park was so clean and tidy the staff were very friendly and helpful , love the text message notifications about fresh bread and meat packs was a awesome idea well done team. We have rebooked for next year this is our new Christmas holiday destination.

I had a beautiful stay at Marion Bay Caravan Park! The park has everything you need for a perfect getaway not to mention the staff are super friendly and accommodating. Would definitely recommend this park to anyone visiting the area. Will definitely be back for a longer stay next time!!!

Ive been attending this caravan park for the last 30 years, The caravan park itself is nice, but the manages were absolutely horrible, zero people skills, rude, treated us very poorly, there were one set of rules for us. And another set of rules for others, we will not be returning to this park while the current managers are still there, hopefully others dont get treated as poorly and unfair as we were,

Visit the park yearly and my family including young children absolutely love it. The Managers are sensational, always friendly and accomodating. They have even gone that extra mile and ensured our cabin’s air con was turned on prior to arrival on a scorching hot day. Amazing stay every time.

Lovely caravan park in a great position with walks, scenic drives and National Park right there. Sites are good size, camp kitchen is like a little community hub, amenities good.

They managed to squeeze us in over New Years at short notice and everyone at reception was always lovely and helpful - thanks for the great tips for our local explorations. We had a nice chat with the owners about our dogs too - they are dog people :)
We found this to be a very chill caravan park, overall well taken care of and with a lovely atmosphere. They send some helpful text messages throughout the day. Apart from the pretty common ‘please drive slowly’ (many caravan parks send standard messages like that these days, bit weird that some reviewers here and on TikTok took them as a personal attack???), they would also let you know when for instance fresh bread was available at reception - much appreciated. They also organised some relaxed live music on the lawn on New Years which was not intrusive but again created a nice community get together. Thank you!
Really lovely park and super accommodating care takers that managed to find a spot for us and our 2 dogs over NY. Great size sites and the amenities were clean. Would definitely recommend

TL;DR
- The owner was immediately aggressive and rude before we were even able to have a conversation.
- We were told twice over the phone that a second car was allowed if we paid extra (which we did) then told the opposite on arrival.
- No solutions or reasonable discussion were offered; only hostility.
- Other guests were allowed two cars on nearby sites, while we were denied the same.

Full version
We were happy with our stay at Marion Bay Caravan Park for the first two nights — staff were friendly and facilities were good. Unfortunately, that changed completely due to the owners’ behaviour.
Before our visit, both my friend and I called twice to confirm rules. We were told the $50/night powered site covered up to six people, one car and one caravan. We then called again prior to arrival as we wanted to bring a second car, we were told we could do so by paying an extra $20 per person per night, which we agreed to and organised in advance.

On arrival, our friends attempted to enter with their car and the owner immediately became aggressive, yelling that a second car was not allowed. There was no calm or respectful conversation, just hostility from the start. When we explained we had been told otherwise over the phone, her husband responded with “that attitude will get you nowhere,” and they were both incredibly aggressive.
We wanted to at least find a solution as we were frustrated that we had pre-organised this. We asked to briefly drive in to unload and then take the car back out 20mins max, this was refused. They then told us “under no circumstances was there to be two cars in at once”. We would need to pack up our awning etc, drive one car out and swap them. Several hours later we saw other campers nearby with two cars allowed on one site. We then asked to pay for an additional site, the female owner aggressively refused without checking, despite multiple empty sites being visible later on.

The inconsistency, lack of communication, and outright rudeness made the rest of our stay uncomfortable.
For the price we paid for a single powered site, we may as well have booked a cabin. We won’t be returning after being treated so poorly. Honestly the car thing wouldn’t have been an issue if the owner actually talked to us like human beings, and also hadn’t told us something totally different on the phone.

*photo attached of two cars on the same site*
Has a big jumpy bouncy thingy i think
Great caravan park,  friendly staff , clean cabins n grounds . Perfect stay for trips around dhillbah nat park . Good squid , good surf , good salmon fishing off white hut .
Clean. Well maintained.  Good camp kitchen. Good drive thru site.
Boutique Park on the beach.  Clean and modern facilities.

Stayed in ensuite cabin - simple accommodation but all that we needed for 2 people - but please provide proper knifes.  the crap plastic things are useless.

Another stay in patio cabins.
Knives still useless, no tv even when a smart tv installed.
Got text the day before departure thanking us for our stay.... Like, we still had one more night.
Was an ok place to stay while exploring but had some issues like ovens in the cabins not working good one cabin air con not working, cabins not cleaned propley.
Friendly service, clean and modern facilities and full reception on everything. There is beach access from the park but it's quite a drop off due to the recent storm erosion.
Just a peaceful place to stay
Great location right on the beach, we stayd in a dog friendly cabin in winter on our trip across from Perth to Adelaide, the Nation Park is a great scenic drive 🚗

The worst caravan park I have been to. Stayed in a cabin dirty floor, sliding door comes off its hinge when trying to close or open, tv doesn't work, WiFi slow can't use it, no good instructions for stove which takes one hour to heat then not hot enough. Very dirty all around inside floor. Only one car allowed with a cabin otherwise you park outside caravan park which is filled with water and mud. Have to carry all your things to car park outside as one car is already inside. The worst place in south Australia and will never recommend it to anyone.
Nice park,friendly staff  good beach access.
Wonderful service really helpful and clean place
Location fabulous.  But not allowed to put a tent up on their precious grass.  Amenities ok.  Lots of rules.

Great place to stay. Shower and toilets Spotless.
Thanks, Dean and Josephine

We had a powered site for two days. We appreciated the generous space between sites for the larger cars. Friendly staff, very clean amenities. Plenty of fishing on the beach and great access to the national park.

Bit disappointing. Service was nice enough but definitely don’t arrive early. Office is not staffed from 10-12 and no one answers the phone! Sites were dusty with no grass where we were. Amenities were ok. Great take off point to explore around the national park.

Beautiful natural beach, easy access from park
Lovely friendly staff
Good facilities
Good coffee from the front office
Good caravan Park
Right on the beach. Amenities are spotless. This place is amazing.
Great Park well maintained,friendly helpful staff, facilities very clean and organised,jumping thing for kids,beautiful camp kitchen,spacious with everything you need.

We were greeted with a great welcome by the management.
The surroundings are fantastic loved the site we had.
A great base to explore the surrounding area
We are looking forward to returning
Great spot when visiting the Dhilba Guuranda-Innes National Park. Big open spaces and good clean facilities.
Beach front on one of the most picturesque bays on the whole peninsula

Marion Bay Caravan Park is a great place to spend the long weekend with family. Children enjoyed a lot at the beach. We can also do paddle-boarding. Morning and evening were serene. The facilities are decent. Overall a good camping experience.

Great spot, right on the beach.
Fantastic facilities,  clean and well maintained.

Stay 3 nights in a cabin with
No working lights
Had to run an extension cord to use microwave
No working stove

Would not recommend

Fairly large spaces for a powered site, so you're not sitting right next to your neighbours. Facilities were clean and easily accessible.
Don was super helpful, accommodated our family across 3 sites and made sure we were together.
We stayed over Australia Day long weekend and there was live music and a food truck.
Will definitely be coming back! Thanks for having us!

Nice camping close to the national park. Amenities are good but little bit dirty at the end of the day. Good for family but not for backpackers I guess, the girl was not really friendly with us for no reason, that’s why I only put 2 stars

Great little park to stay at !  Great little vibe with plenty of things to do around the area. Over the Xmas / New Year period, they have extra activities that you can participate in (Kid friendly too). Good ol Yorkes has some of the best Parks !
This park has absolutely everything you could possible need whether you are in a van, tent, or cabin. Everything is in excellent condition.

We had a powered site for the weekend. We appreciated the generous space between sites for the cars. Friendly staff, very clean amenities. Plenty of bbqs around the park and large indoor area with kitchen. We will definitely come back here again.

Very well located
Good facilities
love this place, our home away from home ❤️
Spent one night,great park and friendly staff.

Marion Bay is our happy place. We've been visiting the Yorke for 5 years now and have called Marion Bay CP home for most of that.

Amenities are top notch and always kept in immaculate condition, and the little extras offered throughout the park are always welcome.

While we stayed in 2023, there were a number of coffee and food vans onsite in the park, meaning that morning coffee duties were taken care of by the professionals, and we had some very tasty pizzas  all provided onsite by The Roaming Stallion.

100% recommend you get in touch with the team and book your stay ASAP!

With easy access to arguably some of the finest beaches Australia has to offer, the park is the ideal base camp to explore the nearby national parks and spectacular coastline on offer.
Great caravan park to stay for a few days whilst exploring the SYP. The staff were really friendly and helpful and the amenities spotless
Roomy,really good amenities,close to beach and friendly staff.
Beautiful caravan park

Our 2 night stay was marred by unfortunate circumstances. Upon arriving our cabin smelt like sewage, and despite opening windows and putting the fan on we couldn’t get ride of the smell. It was freezing cold outside, so we couldn’t keep windows and fan going all night.
Unfortunately due to checking in after normal hours, and having to be up early and back late from a charter meant we were unable to mention the issue to reception, and thought we would just put up with it. On the second night after taking showers our toilet and plumbing backed up, with the toilet almost overflowing. This meant we were unable to have showers or use the toilet that evening or the following morning.
The shower also had a hole in the floor covered in some kind of filler, but it did not feel safe or stable standing on it.
When mentioning the issues to reception I was replied with “I’ll add them to the list to get looked at” ….?
No apology, or notion of concern that we spent two nights breathing in sewage smell and being unable to use our basic amenities we paid for.
It’s a nice spot and location, and the general campaign amenities looked decent, though the showers there were also out of action.
Not a great experience.

A very enjoyable stay for us and our friends, thanks to the Travel Auctions site.
The park is well laid out, sites accessible, good clean ablutions and camp kitchen. Great communal fire pit in season.
Clean and comfortable.
We had a powered site which was spacious with a concrete slab. Camp kitchen or amenities looked like they hadn't been cleaned in the 5 days we were there.
Fantastic caravan park on the tip of Yorke peninsula. Close to Innes National Park and fantastic natural beauty and beautiful sandy beaches. Helpful and friendly staff.
Lovely CP spaced out, great facilities a real gem

Very nice. Clean and well maintained.
Smiled at having two big toilet paper dispensers in the cubicle.

The a sign saying.

If getting low on toilet paper give us  attention

Meanwhile, l recommend this C P.

Even though I didn't catch a blowy off the jetty.
Lovely location. Had a very quiet week. Site was a good size.
A good caravan park with good facilities and green grass areas. Has the usual caravan park facilities . All good. Jim
Visited people staying there. Amenities were new & clean but that's about it. Gravel dirt sites, they have artificial grass pads per site. The manager is very arrogant & unhelpful.

LOVING our stay at the Marion Bay CP at the moment.
We are on the lush green lawned area surrounded by shady trees.
Amenities are fantastic and very clean.
Beautiful clean beach.
The staff are very friendly and accommodating.
Finding it difficult to find a negative!
Thanks for a great stay :)
Very clean tidy has one of the best Fish Cleaning Stations

We originally weren’t going to stay at a caravan park wanted to camp along the Yorke in the bush camping however things don’t go as planned and came to visit Marion bay and by god im glad I did. We visited without a booking and the staff were so accommodating and lovely took us straight away ended up staying two nights and I’m so glad we did. Thanks for such a great time guys.

Quick stay, one night
Park had good facilities and in a lovely location
Owners came over for a drink and assisted with the fish filleting

5 stars for this park

An absolute gem on the Yorke Peninsula!
Right on the beach! Large, level sites, Beautiful lush grass, new and clean ammenities and a super friendly team operating the place! We can’t wait to get back there again soon 😃 Thanks Jarred and Nikki for creating and maintaining a wonderful place to park 👌🏼👌🏼

I usually refrain from leaving reviews as I understand the impact they can have on businesses. However, my recent experience at Marion Bay Caravan Park compels me to share my story to prevent others from facing the same issues. My initial plan was to stay for just Thursday night, but we enjoyed the area so much that we decided to extend our stay. Unfortunately, this is where our troubles began.

Despite multiple attempts to contact the park's management to extend our booking, we received no response throughout the day. As nightfall approached, the lack of communication left us anxious and scrambling to find alternative accommodation, fearing we would have nowhere to sleep. We finally managed to secure a spot at another park but had to rush back to pack up and leave Marion Bay in a hurry.

Upon our departure, the situation escalated when the staff at Marion Bay not only insisted we pay for an additional night due to our late checkout but also accused us of theft when we refused to pay for a night we were not going to use. This accusation was both shocking and unwarranted, especially considering we were actively trying to vacate the site to respect their policies.

This experience was far from what I expected. Not only was the lack of communication a significant inconvenience, but the manner in which the situation was handled upon departure was both unprofessional and unjust. It's with a heavy heart that I share this review, as I believe no one should have to endure such treatment. I hope this feedback encourages Marion Bay Caravan Park to improve their communication and customer service, ensuring future guests do not have to go through a similar ordeal.

What a wonderful little park right near the water,
The staff are fabulous here and will do anything they can to assist you in your stay here.
Right outside of the fabulous Innes National Park too.
What a beautiful park. Nikki and Jarrad were amazing managers and made our stay relaxing and enjoyable. Nothing was too much hassle. Highly recommend staying there when on the YP

We had a great stay here. Beautiful location, right on the beach, walk to the jetty, short drive to National park. Owners were so lovely, even doing a free pancake brekkie one morning which our kids loved. The facilities were clean. Will definitely be back soon.
We loved our stay at Marion Bay CP. Friendly staff, well maintained, clean and tidy. Great beach access, too. Thank you!
Very nice place to go and visit 😊 😀 🙂 ☺️ j
Stayed in December2023  last week. Very tidy and neatly maintained.

Super friendly guys with great pancake and coffee. Highly recommended 👍
The days we stayed were windy but like it😊

I would give this place a -100, only giving it 1 star to put this review. Horrible Managers, very disrespectful. I got a feeling that they were racist towards me and my children. The managers yelled at my children for being “too loud” at the playground when there were other white kids making the same amount of noise. Place is not as clean as expected. Will not be going back ever again.

I am so shamed of humanity because of this park. Recently I came down here with my disabled wife who has speech impairment. the staff were aware of this but still chose ignorance when my wife was made fun of by the other visitors. The manager, Jarred, was extremely rude when I asked for any assistance when I asked for help with technology. I would not recommend this park as I also found the place very unclean and unhygienic.
welcoming and laid back. Fake grass at your site...helps to keep the camper trailer clean
Wonderful caravan park with all the amenities in a perfect location. Marion Bay is a great place to have a holiday.
Great caravan park with super amenities, close to beach, playarea for kids, campkitchen, clean showers, helpful staff!!
It was a bit expensive but a nice position.

I rang twice to book but it went to answer phone call each time where I left a message, but no one got back to me. So went to Edithburgh instead- they were very helpful. Very disappointed in the Marion Bay service - won’t be going back again.
Very clean and tidy park with plenty of very well-kept amenities

We have stayed at many caravan parks and have met many good managers. Jarryd and Nikki have taken park management to a new level. The park is well managed with good facilities. They are super-friendly and helpful. They ensured our stay was of the highest order. Highly recommended. Nikki even gave us a hand to clean and fillet this lot. Sharon & Terrey
